data.content:
### Install Payload Postgres Adapter

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/packages/db-postgres/README.md

Installs the official Payload Postgres adapter using npm, adding it as a dependency to your project. This command fetches the package from the npm registry and adds it to your `node_modules` directory and `package.json`.

```bash
npm install @payloadcms/db-postgres
```

--------------------------------

### Reinstall Project Dependencies with pnpm

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/troubleshooting/troubleshooting.mdx

After removing `node_modules` and updating `package.json` to pin exact versions, use this command to perform a clean reinstallation of all project dependencies. This ensures a fresh and consistent dependency resolution.

```bash
pnpm install
```

--------------------------------

### Install Core Payload 3.0 Dependencies (PNPM)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/migration-guide/overview.mdx

Installs the essential packages for Payload 3.0, including Next.js, React, and core Payload components. This command ensures that all critical dependencies are available and version-synced for the application to function correctly.

```bash
pnpm i next react react-dom payload @payloadcms/ui @payloadcms/next
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload GraphQL Dependency with pnpm

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/graphql/graphql-schema.mdx

This command installs the `@payloadcms/graphql` package as a development dependency using pnpm. It is required to enable GraphQL schema generation capabilities within your Payload CMS project.

```bash
pnpm add @payloadcms/graphql -D
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload MongoDB Adapter

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/packages/db-mongodb/README.md

This command installs the official MongoDB adapter for Payload using npm, adding it as a dependency to your project. Run this in your project's root directory.

```bash
npm install @payloadcms/db-mongodb
```

--------------------------------

### Install Stripe Package for Payload CMS Ecommerce Plugin

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/ecommerce/payments.mdx

This command installs the `stripe` npm package, which is a required dependency for integrating Stripe payment processing with the Payload CMS Ecommerce Plugin. Ensure you install version `18.5.0` or higher for optimal compatibility.

```bash
  pnpm add stripe
```

--------------------------------

### Manage Optional Sharp Dependency for Image Processing (PNPM & TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/migration-guide/overview.mdx

Installs the `sharp` package, which is now an optional dependency for Payload 3.0's image processing features like `formatOptions`, `imageSizes`, or `resizeOptions`. After installation, `sharp` must be explicitly added to the `payload.config.ts` to enable its functionality.

```bash
pnpm i sharp
```

```typescript
// payload.config.ts
import sharp from 'sharp'
buildConfig({
// ...
+   sharp,
})
```

--------------------------------

### Deduplicate pnpm Packages

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/troubleshooting/troubleshooting.mdx

Use `pnpm dedupe` as a final step to flatten the dependency tree and ensure that only a single version of a package is installed where possible. This can help resolve lingering dependency conflicts.

```bash
pnpm dedupe
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload SQLite Adapter via npm

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/packages/db-sqlite/README.md

This command installs the official SQLite adapter package for Payload CMS using npm, adding it to your project's dependencies.

```bash
npm install @payloadcms/db-sqlite
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload Vercel Postgres Adapter (NPM)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/packages/db-vercel-postgres/README.md

Installs the official Payload Vercel Postgres database adapter package using npm. This command adds the adapter as a dependency to your project, enabling integration with Vercel Postgres.

```bash
npm install @payloadcms/db-vercel-postgres
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload Stripe Plugin

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/plugins/stripe.mdx

Installs the Payload Stripe plugin using a JavaScript package manager. This command adds the plugin to your project's dependencies, making it available for configuration.

```bash
pnpm add @payloadcms/plugin-stripe
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload Multi-Tenant Plugin using pnpm

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/packages/plugin-multi-tenant/README.md

This command installs the `@payloadcms/plugin-multi-tenant` package, adding multi-tenancy capabilities to your Payload project. It uses `pnpm` to efficiently add the plugin as a development dependency.

```bash
pnpm add @payloadcms/plugin-multi-tenant
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload Admin Bar package

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/packages/admin-bar/README.md

Installs the `@payloadcms/admin-bar` package using pnpm. This package is a dependency for using the Payload Admin Bar React component in your project.

```bash
pnpm i @payloadcms/admin-bar
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload D1 SQLite Adapter using npm

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/packages/db-d1-sqlite/README.md

This command installs the `@payloadcms/db-d1-sqlite` package via npm, adding it as a project dependency. It is the first step to integrate Cloudflare D1 SQLite with your Payload CMS application.

```bash
npm install @payloadcms/db-d1-sqlite
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Next.js with Payload Plugin for Compatibility

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/getting-started/installation.mdx

To ensure compatibility with packages like `mongodb` or `drizzle-kit` used by Payload, the `withPayload` plugin must wrap your `nextConfig` in `next.config.js`. This function is an ECMAScript module, requiring your Next.js config to be set up for ESM, either by adding `"type": "module"` to `package.json` or by using the `.mjs` file extension.

```js
import { withPayload } from '@payloadcms/next/withPayload'

/** @type {import('next').NextConfig} */
const nextConfig = {
  // Your Next.js config here
  experimental: {
    reactCompiler: false,
  },
}

// Make sure you wrap your `nextConfig`
// with the `withPayload` plugin
export default withPayload(nextConfig)
```

--------------------------------

### Install SEO Plugin (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/plugins/seo.mdx

Install the SEO plugin using a JavaScript package manager such as pnpm, npm, or Yarn. This command adds the necessary package to your project dependencies.

```bash
pnpm add @payloadcms/plugin-seo
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload Slate Rich Text Editor via npm

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rich-text/slate.mdx

This command installs the `@payloadcms/richtext-slate` package, which provides the deprecated Slate-based rich text editor for Payload CMS. It should be saved as a production dependency.

```bash
npm install --save @payloadcms/richtext-slate
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload Cloud Storage Plugin with pnpm

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/packages/plugin-cloud-storage/README.md

This command installs the Payload Cloud Storage plugin package using pnpm, a fast, disk-space efficient package manager. It adds the plugin to your project's dependencies, making it available for integration into your Payload configuration.

```bash
pnpm add @payloadcms/plugin-cloud-storage
```

--------------------------------

### Install core Payload packages in an existing app with pnpm

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/getting-started/installation.mdx

Installs the essential Payload CMS packages, including the core Payload library, Next.js integration, Lexical rich text editor, image processing with sharp, and GraphQL. This command uses pnpm; users should substitute with their preferred package manager (e.g., npm, yarn) and consider `--legacy-peer-deps` for npm if needed.

```bash
pnpm i payload @payloadcms/next @payloadcms/richtext-lexical sharp graphql
```

--------------------------------

### Install Form Builder Plugin

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/plugins/form-builder.mdx

Install the Payload Form Builder plugin using a JavaScript package manager like pnpm, npm, or Yarn. This command adds the necessary package to your project dependencies.

```bash
pnpm add @payloadcms/plugin-form-builder
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload Resend Email Adapter with PNPM

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/packages/email-resend/README.md

Installs the `@payloadcms/email-resend` package using pnpm. This package is a dependency for integrating Resend's email services with a Payload CMS project.

```sh
pnpm add @payloadcms/email-resend
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload Cloud Plugin using Yarn

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/packages/payload-cloud/README.md

This command adds the Payload Cloud plugin package to your project's dependencies. It's the first step to integrating Cloudflare-backed S3 storage, email delivery, and CDN caching into your Payload CMS instance.

```Shell
yarn add @payloadcms/payload-cloud
```

--------------------------------

### Install MongoDB Database Adapter for Payload with pnpm

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/getting-started/installation.mdx

This command installs the official MongoDB adapter for Payload CMS, enabling your application to connect and interact with a MongoDB database. It uses pnpm for package management; replace pnpm with your package manager if different.

```bash
pnpm i @payloadcms/db-mongodb
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload Ecommerce Plugin with pnpm

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/ecommerce/overview.mdx

This command installs the Payload CMS Ecommerce plugin using the pnpm package manager. It adds the `@payloadcms/plugin-ecommerce` package to your project's dependencies, making its functionalities available for integration.

```bash
pnpm add @payloadcms/plugin-ecommerce
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload Sentry Plugin

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/plugins/sentry.mdx

Install the Sentry plugin for Payload using any JavaScript package manager. This command adds the `@payloadcms/plugin-sentry` package to your project's dependencies, enabling Sentry integration.

```bash
pnpm add @payloadcms/plugin-sentry
```

--------------------------------

### Check Payload UI Package Duplicates with pnpm

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/troubleshooting/troubleshooting.mdx

Use this command to inspect the dependency tree for the `@payloadcms/ui` package. It helps identify if multiple versions or duplicate installations of the package exist in your project, which can lead to runtime errors.

```bash
pnpm why @payloadcms/ui
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload Slate Rich Text Editor Package

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/packages/richtext-slate/README.md

This command installs the `@payloadcms/richtext-slate` package, which provides the Slate-based rich text editor functionality for Payload CMS projects. Run this command in your project's terminal to add the dependency.

```bash
npm install @payloadcms/richtext-slate
```

--------------------------------

### Install Redis KV Adapter for Payload using pnpm

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/packages/kv-redis/README.md

This command installs the `@payloadcms/kv-redis` package using pnpm, making it available for use in your Payload project. It's a required step to integrate Redis as a Key-Value adapter.

```sh
pnpm add @payloadcms/kv-redis
```

--------------------------------

### Add Custom Dependency to Import Map

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/custom-components/overview.mdx

Shows how to manually add a custom component or dependency to Payload's import map. This is useful for plugin authors or when a component is not in a standard location, specifying its path, type, and client-side props.

```typescript
import { buildConfig } from 'payload'

export default buildConfig({
  // ...
  admin: {
    // ...
    dependencies: {
      myTestComponent: {
        path: '/components/TestComponent.js#TestComponent',
        type: 'component',
        clientProps: {
          test: 'hello',
        },
      },
    },
  },
})
```

--------------------------------

### Install Nodemailer Email Adapter for PayloadCMS

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/packages/email-nodemailer/README.md

This command installs the `@payloadcms/email-nodemailer` package and the `nodemailer` library using pnpm. These packages are essential for integrating Nodemailer-based email functionality into a PayloadCMS project.

```sh
pnpm add @payloadcms/email-nodemailer nodemailer
```

--------------------------------

### Install Stripe SDK package for Payload CMS

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/ecommerce/plugin.mdx

Before using the Stripe adapter in Payload, the Stripe SDK package needs to be installed manually, as Payload does not automatically include it. This command uses pnpm to add the 'stripe' package to your project.

```shell
pnpm add stripe
```

--------------------------------

### Manually Check Payload UI Package Duplicates in node_modules

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/troubleshooting/troubleshooting.mdx

This command manually searches `node_modules` for `package.json` files that declare `@payloadcms/ui`. This method works with any package manager to detect potential duplicate installations or different versions pointing to the same package.

```bash
find node_modules -name package.json \
     -exec grep -H '"name": "@payloadcms/ui"' {} \; 
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload GCS Storage Plugin

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/packages/storage-gcs/README.md

This shell command adds the `@payloadcms/storage-gcs` package as a dependency to your project using pnpm. This plugin is essential for enabling Google Cloud Storage integration within Payload CMS.

```sh
pnpm add @payloadcms/storage-gcs
```

--------------------------------

### Install PayloadCMS React Live Preview Package

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/live-preview/server.mdx

This command installs the necessary npm package, `@payloadcms/live-preview-react`, required for integrating PayloadCMS server-side live preview functionality into React-based applications.

```bash
npm install @payloadcms/live-preview-react
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload Lexical Rich Text Editor Package

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/packages/richtext-lexical/README.md

This command installs the `@payloadcms/richtext-lexical` package using npm, which provides the Lexical Rich Text Editor plugin for Payload CMS. This is a prerequisite for using the editor in your Payload project.

```bash
npm install @payloadcms/richtext-lexical
```

--------------------------------

### Install MCP Plugin using package manager

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/plugins/mcp.mdx

Command to install the @payloadcms/plugin-mcp package using pnpm. This plugin can also be installed with npm or Yarn package managers. The plugin is currently in Beta and available on npm.

```bash
pnpm add @payloadcms/plugin-mcp
```

--------------------------------

### Install Uploadthing Storage for Payload

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/packages/storage-uploadthing/README.md

This command installs the `@payloadcms/storage-uploadthing` package using pnpm, making it available for integration into your Payload project.

```sh
pnpm add @payloadcms/storage-uploadthing
```

--------------------------------

### Install Postgres Database Adapter for Payload with pnpm

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/getting-started/installation.mdx

This command installs the official PostgreSQL adapter for Payload CMS, allowing your application to connect and interact with a PostgreSQL database. It uses pnpm for package management; ensure to use your package manager if it's not pnpm.

```bash
pnpm i @payloadcms/db-postgres
```

--------------------------------

### Clean pnpm Global Store

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/troubleshooting/troubleshooting.mdx

If dependency issues persist, this command prunes the `pnpm` global content addressable store. It removes unused packages from the store, which can help resolve deeply cached or corrupted dependency states.

```bash
pnpm store prune
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload Nested Docs Plugin

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/plugins/nested-docs.mdx

This command installs the `@payloadcms/plugin-nested-docs` package using pnpm, a JavaScript package manager, making the plugin available for use in your Payload CMS project. Alternative package managers like npm or Yarn can also be used.

```bash
pnpm add @payloadcms/plugin-nested-docs
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload Vercel Content Link Plugin (npm)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/integrations/vercel-content-link.mdx

Install the `@payloadcms/plugin-csm` plugin using npm to enable Vercel Content Link functionality in your Payload project. This plugin is required for embedding Content Source Maps.

```bash
npm i @payloadcms/plugin-csm
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload Redirects Plugin

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/plugins/redirects.mdx

This snippet demonstrates how to install the Payload Redirects Plugin using a JavaScript package manager like pnpm. It's the essential first step to integrating redirect management into your Payload application.

```bash
pnpm add @payloadcms/plugin-redirects
```
